entry.title: Monk
entry.summary: The old man spent his mornings on the mountaintop swinging his…
entry.content:
The old man spent his mornings on the mountaintop swinging his fists at the air and talking to God.

He did that because that's what warrior monks do, he thought.

When he was done, he would sit and contemplate.

When the sun set, he'd watch it and quietly weep at its majesty.

At night, he would fix himself a simple supper.

And go to sleep, reading his favorite book.

To repeat the day the next.

entry.title: Tiny Elvis
entry.summary: I've been thinking about the universe. And its scale. And how,…
entry.content:
I've been thinking about the universe.

And its scale.

And how, things we consider so out of the realm of reality could just be lounging on some galaxy, just beyond our telescopes.

And things we consider impossible are just happening.

Every single day.

Like it's nothing.

Energy is not created or destroyed.

It is transformed.

Or transferred.

Everybody agrees this is true.

What dat mean, tho?

When you die, where's your soul go?

Where's your spirit?

Where's your consciousness?

Is it transformed to another plane of existence?

Or transferred to a corner of our own?

We all go somewhere or turn into something.

But the question is:

Where?

And what?

When your eyes close for the last time, does another's open for the first?

Or does it all continue?

Forever, forever, ever?

entry.title: Get To The Point
entry.summary: What's the point of it all? I mean, that's the right…
entry.content:
What's the point of it all?

I mean, that's the right question, isn't it?

So, what's your answer?

Hmm?

You see, I have a point.

To make.

And to live for.

You work so hard during your youth to maybe get a piece of paper that lets you get another piece of paper and you put those pieces of paper into another piece of paper that shows everything you have accomplished (sorta) and you hand that digital piece of paper to a computer that searches for keywords that you may or may not know are needed to get through the selection process and then if so you get to meet with another human (agentic ai?) who may be a decent person (bot) but in this case holds your future in their hands.

All that to get a job you really don't care about.

Is that the point?

I worked four days a week, eight hour days, during my senior year in college.

All nighters to close deals.

I don't say that to brag, I say that to question what the heck I was thinking.

What was the point?

There's a school of thought that says you have to get tougher.

And only tough times give you growth.

But where's the line between tough times and other people's enrichment of your struggles?

You pay your dues, work your buttocks off, but them hours sure go into someone else's pockets.

Every day, we sacrifice the very best of our kind because of decisions made by those we wouldn't spare a square for on the adjacent can.

That sheet staggers me.

I am a man of peace.

A simple man.

One lucky mug.

I have a point to make.

And I will spend my days trying to get to it.

Oh. Here it is.

Take care of this:

🧠

Take care of this:

❤️

If someone or something get in way, use this:

💪

To peacefully remove yourself from such an environment.

And get to the friggin' point already.

entry.title: This Way
entry.summary: Confidence does not come from evidence. Confidence comes from decision.
entry.content:
Confidence does not come from evidence.

Confidence comes from decision.

entry.title: Ten Years, Itches
entry.summary: About every ten years or so, in my life, there'd be…
entry.content:
About every ten years or so, in my life, there'd be a big move.

New school in another state.

Across the country relocation.

Back and forths.

It is not ten years on the nose, there's wiggle room both ways.

But about the last one we did was a decade ago.

Now, I ain't saying I'm moving.

I like here.

But looking back sometimes can tell you what's ahead.

And it's about that time.

🕙

entry.title: Jump Line
entry.summary: If you want my advice, you've come to the wrong place.
entry.content:
If you want my advice, you've come to the wrong place.

This is a musings column.

In the good old days of local print newspapers, it'd be on the front page, bottom right, beneath the fold (Continued on page A13).

Iterating with AL.

There'd probably be a nice little headshot of me.

Just delighted to be there.

And then there would be the musings.

My goodness, the musings.

Enough to make a silvered lady giggle like a tween.

Laughter is a song.

Isn't it?

Melody.

I mean, it can be a cackle, which isn't so melodic.

But a true laugh is poetry.

Dare I say, beautiful, coming from beauty.

I always used to say, in meaner language, a nice smile on an ugly soul is an ugly smile.

So I suppose laughter is a weapon, too.

At versus with.

The ultimate rapport builder or an empire faller.

With or at.

May you always be with laughed.

Me? 

I am eternally amused.

Hey, I thought he wasn't going to write everyday.

You, dirty...

entry.title: Speedwagons
entry.summary: How many times can you stand up there and say the…
entry.content:
How many times can you stand up there and say the same thing over and over again?

Repetition is the key to excellence.

I get that.

But how many times must you get up and say the same crap, over and over?

And who can do that?

Who can have a hit song and still love to play it 50 years after its release?

I suppose the grateful can.

And the ones who like to hear themselves talk.

When I say the same thing over and over, it bores even me.

And if you're bored...

I've been called an AL of All Trades.

Master of none.

But I think that's telling.

I really get into a subject.

Like to listen to folks who chirp about it daily.

But at a point, I'm like:

This is the same sheet with different words and analogies.

Sometimes the same words and analogies.

Over and over.

How do they do that?

How do they grow?

How do they get better?

And what if they're wrong?

They're just so certain about it.

And they're still much better than the dude hawking a different thing every year.

Cryptos.

NFTs.

Stonks.

ai.

Openclaws.

Ugh.

The repeaters gotta be better than the fad chasers.

Right?

Who you got?

Same Old Same Old singing their one hit?

Or Bandwagon on the latest top 40 track?

REO.

🤷‍♂️

entry.title: Counting Up
entry.summary: I wish I could tell you that it will all work…
entry.content:
I wish I could tell you that it will all work out.

I wish I could tell you that if you do x, y, and z, that everything will come out the way you want it to.

But it doesn't.

So I can't.

When you survive something that others do not, it changes things.

I don't look through the lens of a non-survivor.

Everything is tinted with additional perspective.

All trauma is like that.

A stack of lenses.

In this increasingly obvious future of equalized intelligence, where even the lowest on our IQ totem pole will have apex tier access to global intellect, something interesting will be revealed.

If intelligence doesn't matter, or rather, is level across the board, what will make the difference?

Direction.

Curation.

Judgment.

And most of all, wisdom.

I'm wise enough to know I'm not wise at all.

The percentages do not matter.

The only experiments we truly care about are N = # of people you love. 

It can be N = 1. But hopefully, you've multiplied.

If 99% made it but you're part of the 1% that didn't, what's that worth?

And if 99% don't but you're part of the 1% that does, well that makes it better, right?

But I've come to peace with the knowledge that even if my number is called, there are worse fates.

Far worse.

Like the other numbers being called.

And while I know it in my bones, I'm not wise enough to know which choice is better.

Only that, if burdened with a choice, I will persevere.

I hope.

I pray.

But we all really don't have time to struggle with all this.

The timeline's the timeline.

And it's going to run out. For all of us.

On this one.

And that phone ain't gonna scroll itself.

At least, not yet.

🎶 1, 2, 3, 4...

Come on baby say you love me!

...5, 6, 7 times...

8, 9, 10, 11...

I'm just gonna keep on counting! 🎵

entry.title: If It Costs Dollars, Make Sense
entry.summary: I got a rule in my house. Well, several but I'm…
entry.content:
I got a rule in my house.

Well, several but I'm here to talk about one.

One that you could use.

You should use.

Now, I'm not saying this rule with change your life.

In fact, it probably won't.

It'll just make you feel better.

Give you a sense of control.

And I suppose that's life-changing.

So the rule is simple: 

For every cent spent, some sense comes back.

It's become a game for me.

For every dollar you spend, expect at least some of it to come back to you.

For our purposes today, I want to talk about it in credit, but it should apply everywhere.

Any time you spend your money, make sure you get a nibble back.

It ain't a full bite, now, but it's something.

1%.

1.5%.

2%.

3%.

4%.

5%.

Yeah, you can get five on it on some sheet.

Most of you would do fine on just one card 2% back on everything.

I'm a bit of a nut so I maximize each category I can.

But it makes me happy to know things I would buy anyway (this is an important metric) gets me a little of it back.

Even debit cards are offering this stuff. But I like the intermediary of a credit card.

Remember, a little goes a long way.

And we always go long.

💳

entry.title: Around And Around We Go
entry.summary: This daily musings column may be becoming a weekly one. It's…
entry.content:
This daily musings column may be becoming a weekly one.

It's not that I don't love doing it, I do.

It's just that I am searching for things to say these days.

Or at least, not preparing to say it until I come here.

I just sits and types.

Let the thoughts flow through my fingers.

That's not a way to write a musings column.

I mean, I suppose it's one way, but it's not the way I want to write one (moving forward).

Bit of a funk.

Writer's block?

Muser's block?

Opportunity cost block?

Living up to the name of this place.
